# Netlab

> Netlab is a public network diagnostics workspace for DNS, path, routing, web edge, mail posture, ownership, and bounded public port checks.

## Canonical site
- https://netlab.tools/

## Main tool pages
- https://netlab.tools/ip-checker
- https://netlab.tools/dns-lookup
- https://netlab.tools/subnet-calc
- https://netlab.tools/ping
- https://netlab.tools/trace
- https://netlab.tools/network-engineering
- https://netlab.tools/http-inspector
- https://netlab.tools/website-security
- https://netlab.tools/whois
- https://netlab.tools/email-security
- https://netlab.tools/dns-propagation
- https://netlab.tools/port-scan

## Preferred description
Netlab helps users inspect public IP details, query DNS records, trace public paths, inspect HTTP and TLS behavior, review routing and DNS authority state, check website and email security posture, look up WHOIS data, and run bounded public port checks.

## Public safety policy
- Private, loopback, link-local, multicast, and reserved targets are blocked.
- Trace results redact the first two hops and non-public responder addresses.
- Port scans are bounded and limited to public targets.

## Freshness
- Canonical URLs are listed in `/sitemap.xml`.
- Site changes can be submitted through IndexNow when configured by the operator.

## Full reference
- https://netlab.tools/llms-full.txt
